mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases, or mean return is not greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.0%, restrictive monetary policy weighs on equity valuations through higher discount rates and slower credit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0%, or mean return is not below zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and consumer prices indicate a stagflationary impulse where monetary policy is chasing inflation; the joint level of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL produces a Rank IC magnitude below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or joint increases fail to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a decline in housing starts signals that rising interest rates are transmitting to the interest-rate-sensitive housing sector, forecasting weaker downstream construction and consumption and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following HOUST declines greater than 5% over 3 months are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is above 3.5% and UNRATE is below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production momentum reflects real-time manufacturing activity; month-over-month increases produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because production surprises revise earnings expectations upward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of weeks when INDPRO increases month-over-month, or mean return is not greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and contracting consumption demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negative in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, or mean return is not below zero (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and payroll employment indicates healthy demand backed by income, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of RSAFS and PAYEMS growth produces a Rank IC magnitude below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns do not occur in more than 55% of joint-increase instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation crosses above 3.5% during a tightening cycle, the Federal Reserve is compelled to maintain or accelerate rate hikes, raising the equity risk premium and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5% during FEDFUNDS above 4.0%, or mean return is not below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
